summary:
CD63 is considered a well-established exosomal membrane protein associated with endocytic pathways; it consistently appears across multiple studies when identifying extracellular vesicles derived from cells under various conditions such as inflammation or disease states like cancer progression where specific subpopulations may show altered expression patterns related to tumor microenvironment interactions.

I agree with the statement because recent findings confirm CD63’s role beyond just being present—it functions actively within exosomes during cargo sorting processes involving other tetraspanins including CD9 and CD81 which enhances specificity in distinguishing true exosomes versus non-exosomal particles released into circulation.

\boxed{\text{Yes}}

The summary above reflects only what was directly stated in Agent1's reasoning without adding external information or interpretation outside those statements.

I agree with the statement because recent findings confirm CD63’s role beyond just being present—it functions actively within exosomes during cargo sorting processes involving other tetraspanins including CD9 and CD81 which enhances specificity in distinguishing true exosomes versus non-exosomal particles released into circulation.

\boxed{\text{Yes}}
